hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 5, A. Your opponent raised before the cards were dealt. You called.
River — 5. Your opponent checked — then called your bet.

wrongReasons: J 5 plays similarly, but the correct hand pairs a specific board card that J 5 doesn't. Look at which hole card ranks actually appear on the board.
6 10 makes a flush here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
With only high card, A 8 wouldn't commit chips across multiple streets. This betting line requires a genuinely made hand from the flop onward.
hand.explanation:
Answer: K K

Your opponent had an overpair and bet the flop and turn for value but checked back the river — a sign they were confident earlier but wanted pot control once the board got scarier.
